# Joshua Swanson

## About
Joshua Swanson is a computer science masters student at ETH Zurich, Switzerland. His major is in Machine Intelligence with a minor in Data Management. He holds a BA in Mathematics and a BSc in Computer Science from the University of Washington. He is originally from the USA.

## Research
His research focuses on AI security and privacy, specifically studying how AI can be misused for malicious purposes, including deanonymization, OSINT, and surveillance.

## Publications

### Large-Scale Online Deanonymization with LLMs (2026)
Authors: Simon Lermen, Daniel Paleka, Joshua Swanson, Michael Aerni, Nicholas Carlini, Florian Tramer
Published at the ICLR 2026 Workshop on Agents in the Wild.
This paper demonstrates that large language models can identify pseudonymous online users at scale with high precision (up to 90%) and at low cost ($1-4 per target). The research was featured in The New York Times, The Guardian, Bloomberg, The Independent, El Pais, The Verge, Ars Technica, Al Jazeera, and many other international outlets.
Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.16800

### Modal Aphasia (2025)
Authors: Joshua Swanson, Fabian Paischer, Florian Tramer
This paper investigates whether unified multimodal language models can describe images from memory.
Paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.19394
